# Garnet v0.4.2 — Mini-Spec v1.0 Conformance Matrix

**As of:** 2026-04-26 (Garnet release v0.4.2)
**Source of truth:** [`GARNET_v1_0_Mini_Spec.md`](GARNET_v1_0_Mini_Spec.md)
**Generated by:** evidence-based audit of the workspace at commit `4798fbf`.

---

## Purpose

This document is a single, honest table answering the question every
serious reader eventually asks: **"What in the Mini-Spec actually
runs today?"**

The answer is not uniform — Garnet's compiler-as-agent architecture
has been built rung-by-rung, and the surface-level grammar runs ahead
of the type system, which runs ahead of the runtime, which runs ahead
of `Sendable`-style formal guarantees. The matrix below makes that
ordering visible per Mini-Spec section so that:

- Users do not show up expecting features that exist on paper but
  not in code.
- Researchers can cite specific implementation status when comparing
  Garnet to other in-progress languages.
- Contributors can see at a glance where the next high-leverage
  patches land.

## Status legend

| Symbol | Meaning |
|---|---|
| ✅ | **Implemented.** Lexed, parsed, checked (where applicable), and executable in the v0.3 interpreter or supported runtime. Has tests. |
| 🔵 | **Parsed-only.** AST nodes exist; parser accepts the syntax; but the checker and/or runtime ignores it (e.g. it parses through but produces no semantic effect). |
| 🟡 | **Partial.** Implemented for the common case; specific spec subsections explicitly deferred. Notes column lists the gap. |
| 🟠 | **Deferred.** Specified in Mini-Spec v1.0 but not in code today. Tracked for v1.1 / v0.5.x. |
| ⚫ | **Not started.** No tokens, no AST, no runtime. |

## Methodology caveats

- "Implemented" means *evidence found in source + test* — not *formally
  verified equivalent to the spec*. Most rows below would benefit from
  a property-based conformance test suite that does not yet exist
  (tracked separately as the Paper VI Empirical Validation Protocol).
- The interpreter (`garnet-interp-v0.3`) is the v0.3 reference
  semantics. Per the engineering ladder, a native compiler is a
  later-rung concern; "runtime" below means "executes in the
  reference interpreter," not "compiles to machine code."
- Where the parser accepts syntax that the runtime cannot execute,
  the row is marked 🔵 *Parsed-only* and the runtime gap is the
  primary thing the next implementation rung must close.

---

## Section 2 — Lexical Grammar

| Mini-Spec | Status | Evidence | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| §2.1 Character set / encoding | ✅ | `garnet-parser-v0.3/src/lexer.rs` | UTF-8 throughout. |
| §2.2 Keywords | ✅ | `garnet-parser-v0.3/src/token.rs` (`Kw*` variants) | All v1.0 keywords tokenized except `KwYield` (see §5.4). |
| §2.3 Operators / precedence | ✅ | `garnet-parser-v0.3/src/grammar/expr.rs` (11-level Pratt tower) | Includes pipeline `\|>`, range `..`, safe-nav. |
| §2.4 Literals | ✅ | `lexer.rs` + `parse_examples` tests | Int/Float/String/Bool/Symbol/Array/Tuple. |

## Section 3 — Module System

| Mini-Spec | Status | Evidence | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| §3.1 File-based modules | ✅ | `garnet-parser-v0.3/src/grammar/modules.rs`, `garnet-interp-v0.3/src/env.rs` | |
| §3.2 Inline modules | ✅ | `parse_modules` tests | |
| §3.3 Imports (`use`) | ✅ | `UseDecl` / `UseImports` in `ast.rs` | |
| §3.4 Visibility (`pub`) | ✅ | `KwPub` token, parser respects on items | Cross-module `pub` enforcement is checker-side and minimal — full visibility regime tracked for v0.5. |

## Section 4 — Memory Units (Memory Core / Mnemos)

The implementation crate `garnet-memory-v0.3/` ships under the codename
**Mnemos** as the v0.4.x reference implementation of Garnet's
**Memory Core**. Production-path work for §4.4 / §4.5 and the
allocator integration is sequenced in
[`MEMORY_CORE_ROADMAP.md`](MEMORY_CORE_ROADMAP.md).

| Mini-Spec | Status | Evidence | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| §4.1 Declaration form | ✅ | `grammar/memory.rs`, `MemoryDecl` AST node | |
| §4.2 Semantics | ✅ | Mnemos: `garnet-memory-v0.3/src/{working,episodic,semantic,procedural}.rs` | Reference stores meeting the behavioural contract; not production allocators. Roadmap Tier 1 + Tier 2 productizes the backends. |
| §4.3 Out-of-scope items | ✅ (by design) | n/a | These remain explicit non-goals. |
| §4.4 Generics over memory kinds | 🟠 | n/a | Explicitly deferred; tracked as Roadmap T1.3 (gates on §11.6 monomorphization). |
| §4.5 ARC + Bacon–Rajan cycle detection | 🟠 | spec only — no implementation file | New in v1.0; tracked as Roadmap T3.1. The single largest open Memory Core item. |
| §4.5.1 Trial-deletion algorithm | 🟠 | — | Roadmap T3.1 |
| §4.5.2 Kind-aware root partitioning | 🟠 | — | Roadmap T3.1 |
| §4.5.3 Finalization | 🟠 | — | Roadmap T3.1 |
| §4.5.4 Safe-mode interaction | 🟠 | — | Roadmap T3.1 / T3.2 |
| §4.5.5 Observable invariants | 🟠 | — | Roadmap T3.1 |

## Section 5 — Function Definitions

| Mini-Spec | Status | Evidence | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| §5.1 Managed-mode (`def`) | ✅ | `grammar/functions.rs`, `interp::eval` | |
| §5.2 Safe-mode (`fn`) | ✅ | `grammar/functions.rs`, `garnet-check-v0.3` | |
| §5.3 Closures (both modes) | ✅ | `ClosureBody` in `ast.rs`, `FnValue` in interp | |
| §5.4 Blocks + `yield` | 🟠 | no `KwYield`, no AST node, no eval | Phase 1B Ruby gap #8. Major v0.5 work — needs token, parser path, eval semantics, and test fixtures. |
| §5.4.1–5.4.5 sub-rules | 🟠 | — | Blocked on §5.4 above. |

| Mini-Spec | Status | Evidence | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| §7.1 Design principle | ✅ | architectural | Dual-mode preserved. |

| §7.3 Safe-mode `Result` + `?` | ✅ | parser + interp + checker | `RescueClause` AST node. |

## Section 8 — Safe-Mode Validation

| Mini-Spec | Status | Evidence | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| §8.1 Security Theorem (capability boundary) | 🟡 | `garnet-check-v0.3/src/caps_graph.rs` (783 lines) | CapCaps propagator implemented; *theorem* is paper-only. |
| §8.2 Mode boundary semantics | ✅ | `garnet-check-v0.3/src/audit.rs` (268 lines) | ModeAuditLog catches every fn↔def crossing. |
| §8.5 NLL / lifetime inference | 🟠 | spec only | Hand-coded for the simplest cases in `borrow.rs`; full NLL deferred. |
| §8.6 Borrow-check rules B1–B5 | 🟡 | `garnet-check-v0.3/src/borrow.rs` (395 lines) | Skeleton; not all five rules formally checked. |

| Mini-Spec | Status | Evidence | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| §9.1 Actor declaration | ✅ | `grammar/actors.rs`, `ActorDef`/`ProtocolDecl`/`HandlerDecl` AST | |

| Mini-Spec | Status | Evidence | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| §10 Boundary crossing audit log | ✅ | `garnet-check-v0.3/src/audit.rs` ModeAuditLog | Closes the "hidden safe→managed escalation" threat class. |

## Section 11 — User Types

| Mini-Spec | Status | Evidence | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| §11.1 `struct` | ✅ | `StructDef`, `FieldDef`, `interp::eval` | Construction + field access. |
| §11.2 `enum` | ✅ | `EnumDef`, `Variant`, pattern match arms | |
| §11.3 `trait` | 🔵 | `TraitDef`, `TraitItem`, `FnSig` AST present | Parsed; runtime dispatch is interp-side basic; no method resolution table. |
| §11.4 `impl` blocks | 🔵 | `ImplBlock` AST | Same status as §11.3. |
| §11.5 Trait coherence (formal orphan-rule algorithm) | 🟠 | spec only | New in v1.0; no enforcement in checker today. |
| §11.6 Monomorphization + Zero-Cost Abstraction Theorem | 🔵 | `type_params: Vec<String>` parsed throughout AST (`fn`/`struct`/`enum`/`trait`/`impl`) | Generics PARSED. No monomorphization — interpreter uses dynamic dispatch only. Theorem applies to a hypothetical future native compiler. |
| §11.7 `@dynamic` method dispatch | 🟠 | no `@dynamic` annotation, no per-instance method table | Phase 1B Ruby gap #9. Needs annotation support, runtime dispatch table, and §11.7.6 safe-mode prohibition check. |
| §11.8 Structural protocols (duck typing) | 🟠 | no `protocol` top-level keyword, no structural cast | Phase 1B Ruby gap #10. Needs grammar, type-side static-structural typing, and runtime cast. |

## Section 15 — REPL

| Mini-Spec | Status | Evidence | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| §15.1–15.10 Full REPL spec | 🟡 | `garnet-interp-v0.3/src/repl.rs` (125 lines) | Basic working REPL with `preload`. Mode switching, history persistence, `:commands` only partly covered. Compare to Ruby's `irb` for the gap list. |

| §16.2 Project layout | ✅ | `garnet-cli/templates/{cli,web-api,agent-orchestrator}` | |

| §16.4 Doc-comment syntax | 🟡 | parser tolerates `///` | No `garnet doc` subcommand yet (tracked in v0.4.2 release-readiness Refactor #7). |
| §16.5 Cross-platform installer | ✅ | `installer/sh.garnet-lang.org/install.sh`, `docs/install.sh` | Linux package paths verified Phase 6D; macOS/Windows installer paths covered. |
| §16.6 Why single-CLI matters | n/a (rationale) | Paper VII | |

## Summary scorecard

| Section | Implemented (✅) | Parsed-only (🔵) | Partial (🟡) | Deferred (🟠) | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| §2 Lexical | 4 | — | — | — | Complete. |
| §3 Modules | 4 | — | — | — | Cross-module visibility audit pending. |
| §4 Memory | 3 | — | — | 7 | ARC cycle detection (§4.5) is the largest single open Mini-Spec deliverable. |
| §5 Functions | 3 | — | — | 6 | `yield`/blocks (§5.4) is the next-largest. |
| §6 Control flow | 3 | — | — | — | Complete. |
| §7 Errors | 3 | — | — | — | Complete. |
| §8 Safe-mode | 1 | — | 3 | — | Skeleton checker; formal theorem stays paper-side. |
| §9 Actors | 3 | — | 1 | — | `Sendable` runtime guard is partial. |
| §10 Boundaries | 1 | — | — | — | Audit log complete. |
| §11 User types | 2 | 3 | — | 3 | Generics parse but don't monomorphize; `@dynamic` and protocols fully deferred. |
| §15 REPL | — | — | 1 | — | Basic working; commands/history pending. |
| §16 Tooling | 4 | — | 1 | — | `garnet doc` is the v0.4.2 Refactor #7 line item. |
| **Totals** | **31** | **3** | **6** | **16** | 56 tracked items. |

## What this matrix is not

- It is not a substitute for the Mini-Spec itself; the spec is
  normative, this is descriptive.
- It is not a roadmap; ordering of 🟠 items belongs in the next phase
  plan, not here.
- It is not a green/red dashboard against a CI gate; the Paper VI
  Empirical Validation Protocol is where pre-registered conformance
  tests will live once they exist.

## How to keep this honest

When you change implementation status — landing `yield`, hooking up
`@dynamic`, finishing the borrow checker — flip the row in this
matrix in the same commit. A stale conformance matrix is worse than
no matrix at all because it pretends to inform.
